supportive_message: Take a moment to check in with yourself. Whatever you're feeling is valid. Start small — one breath, one step, one task at a time.
Your mind is clear but your energy is a bit low. Use this clarity to plan your next steps — you don't need to execute yet, just map it out.
There's a lot going on internally. Writing it out — even just stream of consciousness for 5 minutes — often brings surprising clarity.
You're in a settled state. A good time to review your priorities and plan the next few hours without pressure.
Your body and mind seem to be asking for rest. Listen to that. Even a 20-minute break now will pay back later.
You're in a neutral, stable place. A good moment to gently plan your day without pressure — nothing urgent, just some gentle structure.
You seem a bit unsettled. Try box breathing — inhale 4 counts, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4. Just two rounds can take the edge off.
Things feel like too much right now, and that's okay. Try the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding exercise — name 5 things you can see, 4 you can touch, and so on. It brings you back to the present moment.
Your body has energy that needs somewhere to go. A short walk or 10 minutes of movement will do more for your focus than pushing through right now.
You're carrying a lot right now. Before anything else, let's slow your system down. Try 4-7-8 breathing for just 5 minutes — it directly signals your nervous system to calm. Everything else can wait.

There's a low-level restlessness here. A short yoga flow — even 10 minutes — can channel that energy and leave you clearer.
